content.problemStatement:
You are given a matrix AAA consisting of NNN rows and MMM columns.

You task is to select an element A(x,y)A_{(x, y)}A(x,y)​ (1≤x≤N,1≤y≤M)(1 \le x \le N, 1 \le y \le M)(1≤x≤N,1≤y≤M) such that the following value is **maximised**:


∑i=1,i≠xi=N∑j=1,j≠yj=M(A(i,j)⊕A(x,y))\sum_{i = 1, i\neq x}^{i = N} \sum_{j = 1, j\neq y}^{j = M} (A_{(i, j)} \oplus A_{(x, y)})∑i=1,i=xi=N​∑j=1,j=yj=M​(A(i,j)​⊕A(x,y)​). Here, ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes bitwise XOR operation.
In other words, you need to select an element A(x,y)A_{(x, y)}A(x,y)​ such that the sum of bitwise XOR of A(x,y)A_{(x, y)}A(x,y)​ with all elements that are **not in** the xthx^{th}xth row **or** the ythy^{th}yth column is maximum.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM — the number of rows and columns.
The next NNN lines contains MMM space-separated integers each, describing the entire matrix.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **maximum sum** possible.
constraints: The sum of N×MN \times MN×M over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Consider the element A(2,1)=4A_{(2, 1)} = 4A(2,1)​=4. The sum obtained on selecting this element would be 4⊕2+4⊕3=6+7=134\oplus 2 + 4 \oplus 3 = 6+7 = 134⊕2+4⊕3=6+7=13. Note that the elements of second row and first column are omitted while calculating the sum.
It can proven that no other element would lead to a sum more than 131313.
